feedback.text: I would like to join in on asking to use this opportunity to also proactively stop the destruction of digital games. For far too long we as consumers have had to just hope for publishers to give us the ability to play our purchased games after online services had been shut down. Just as it has been described in the European Citizens Initiative "Stop Destroying Videogames" (https://eci.ec.europa.eu/045/public/#/screen/home) with over a million signatures, we would like to have our rights protected and to prevent the remote disabling of videogames by the publishers, before providing reasonable means to continue functioning of said videogames without the involvement from the side of the publisher. Right now, a publisher can just disable or de-list any game for any reason and leave us with no access, no refund, and no viable alternative. Please consider this in your Act.
